topic: An employee is transferred to a different department
raw_output:
[story 1]

The beige cubicle walls seemed to press inward, suffocating the already thin air. Sarah meticulously re-filed the last of the Q3 reports, her fingers trembling slightly as she tried to align the documents perfectly. The notification email, stark and impersonal, had landed this morning: transfer to the Marketing division. A knot tightened in her chest, a cold, heavy weight that made every breath feel shallow. She stared at the blinking cursor on her screen, the silence of the office suddenly deafening. Every scheduled meeting felt like an impending ambush, and the thought of learning an entirely new set of procedures made her stomach clench. She picked up her worn tote bag, the leather cool against her palm, and walked toward the elevator, each step feeling measured and agonizingly slow.

[story 2]

"Just another shuffle," Mark muttered to himself, running a hand over his closely cropped hair. He kept glancing at the clock on the wall, the seconds stretching into an unbearable expanse. The news of the departmental move had hit him like a physical blow—a sudden, jarring shift in his established routine. He found himself pacing the narrow confines of his desk, his jaw tight enough to ache. He kept replaying yesterday's team meeting in his mind, dissecting every word, searching for some hidden clue that explained this sudden upheaval. When his manager finally called him in, Mark managed a strained, overly bright smile, but his hands were slick with sweat as he tried to formulate a coherent response to the impending reorganization.

[story 3]

Eleanor kept smoothing the lapels of her blazer, a repetitive, almost compulsive motion. The sheer volume of new information looming ahead—different software, different colleagues, entirely unfamiliar objectives—created a buzzing static behind her eyes. She tried to focus on the potted succulent on her windowsill, but her gaze kept drifting to the hallway, imagining the unfamiliar faces she would encounter. When her supervisor explained the logistics of the move, Eleanor nodded too quickly, her voice coming out higher pitched than intended. She gripped the edge of her desk until her knuckles turned white, desperately wishing the floor would simply swallow her whole and offer a quiet reprieve from the overwhelming pressure building inside her.
